steps.title: Use the domain before squaring
steps.content:
Because the left side is a square root, the right side must be nonnegative: $31-x \ge 0$. Let $t=31-x$, so $t \ge 0$ and $x=31-t$. The equation becomes
$$\sqrt{k-(31-t)}=t$$
Now both sides are nonnegative, so squaring preserves the solution condition:
$$k-31+t=t^2$$
$$k=t^2-t+31$$

steps.title: Find the minimum k that gives one solution
steps.content:
Now the number of solutions is the number of nonnegative $t$-values satisfying $k=t^2-t+31$. Complete the square:
$$k=t^2-t+31=\left(t-\frac12\right)^2+\frac{123}{4}$$
The minimum possible value of $k$ is $\frac{123}{4}$, and it occurs at one value, $t=\frac12$. Therefore:
$$4k=4\left(\frac{123}{4}\right)=123$$
The answer is 123.
